examples.title: System V Shared Memory - Producer
examples.description: Demonstrates creating and writing to shared memory as producer process
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates: 1) Using ftok() to generate a key, 2) Creating shared memory with shmget(), 3) Attaching with shmat(), 4) Writing to shared memory, 5) Detaching with shmdt(). In practice, semaphores should be used for synchronization.

examples.title: System V Shared Memory - Consumer
examples.description: Demonstrates reading from shared memory as consumer process
examples.explanation: The consumer uses the same key to access existing shared memory. SHM_RDONLY flag makes the attachment read-only for safety. The process reads the message written by producer.

examples.title: POSIX Shared Memory with mmap
examples.description: Demonstrates POSIX shared memory using shm_open and mmap
examples.explanation: POSIX shared memory uses file-like interface: shm_open() creates the object, ftruncate() sets size, mmap() maps it to address space, munmap() unmaps, and shm_unlink() removes it.

examples.title: Shared Memory with Semaphore Synchronization
examples.description: Demonstrates proper synchronization between processes using semaphores
examples.explanation: This code shows proper synchronization using semaphores. The union semun is required for semctl operations. sem_lock() decrements semaphore (wait), sem_unlock() increments (signal). This prevents race conditions.

examples.title: Anonymous Shared Memory (Parent-Child)
examples.description: Demonstrates anonymous shared memory using mmap for parent-child communication
examples.explanation: MAP_ANONYMOUS creates memory without file backing. Parent and child share the same physical memory after fork(). This is efficient for parent-child IPC. No explicit cleanup of shared memory needed as it's not backed by file or SysV segment.
